description: The flagship example archive on uXu. A curated, growing collection of Grateful Dead live shows (1969–1995), built to demonstrate what's possible on this platform — full shows, best-available recordings, and playback that actually works in the browser. Every show below has been individually verified against Internet Archive and setlist records.

setlist: Set 2: Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain
St. Stephen > Not Fade Away > St. Stephen
shows.notes: Widely regarded as one of the greatest live recordings in rock history. Sourced from Betty Cantor-Jackson's soundboard reels; deemed culturally significant enough to be added to the Library of Congress National Recording Registry in 2011.

setlist: Beat It on Down the Line
China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
Dark Star > Drums > The Other One > Sing Me Back Home
Turn On Your Love Light > Goin' Down the Road Feelin' Bad > Not Fade Away
shows.notes: From the legendary Europe '72 tour. This show was later included in the Europe '72: The Complete Recordings box set, sourced from Charlie Miller's soundboard transfer.

setlist: We Can Run But We Can't Hide
Stuck Inside of Mobile with the Memphis Blues Again
Uncle John's Band > Playing in the Band (reprise)
Dark Star > Drums > Space > Death Don't Have No Mercy > Dear Mr. Fantasy > Hey Jude > Throwing Stones > Good Lovin'
shows.notes: Billed as 'Formerly the Warlocks' to keep a low profile after the band was effectively banned from Hampton following 1988 incidents. This show marked the first Dark Star since July 1984 and the first Attics of My Life since 1972. Released commercially as the box set 'Formerly the Warlocks.'

setlist: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
shows.notes: Known as 'Sunshine Daydream,' a benefit show for the Springfield Creamery (run by Ken Kesey's brother) later released as both a concert film and a live album in 2013. One of the most beloved circulating tapes from 1972, mastered from 16-track soundboard reels.

setlist: Corrina > Drums > Space > Unbroken Chain > Sugar Magnolia
Encore: Black Muddy River > Box of Rain
shows.notes: Jerry Garcia's final live performance with the Grateful Dead, one month before his death on August 9, 1995. The second of two consecutive nights at Soldier Field, capping the band's 30-year career.

setlist: Set 1: Good Morning Little School Girl
Cryptical Envelopment > Drums > The Other One > Cryptical Envelopment
Mountains of the Moon > Dark Star > St. Stephen > The Eleven > Turn On Your Love Light
shows.notes: Part of the run that produced material for the original Live/Dead album. The St. Stephen from this night was selected for the album's final mix. Recorded direct to 16-track by engineers Bob Matthews and Betty Cantor.

setlist: It Must Have Been the Roses
Me and My Uncle > Mexicali Blues
Ramble On Rose > Little Red Rooster
Brown Eyed Women > Looks Like Rain > Deal
Set 3: Don't Ease Me In > Lost Sailor > Saint of Circumstance > Franklin's Tower > Drums > Fire on the Mountain > Not Fade Away > Stella Blue > Goin' Down the Road Feelin' Bad > Good Lovin'
shows.notes: Halloween show from the acoustic/electric 1980 tour, simulcast via FM radio and closed-circuit TV. The acoustic-set format was a first for the band since 1970.

setlist: Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain
Set 3: Dark Star > The Other One > Dark Star > Wharf Rat > St. Stephen > Good Lovin'
shows.notes: The farewell concert for Winterland Arena, simulcast on KSAN-FM radio and KQED television, with Dan Aykroyd leading the midnight countdown and guest spots from John Cipollina and Ken Kesey. Officially released in 2003 as the 4-disc set 'The Closing of Winterland,' mixed from the original 24-track masters.
